root_text.sn20.5:1.4: ‘ahaṁ imaṁ sattiṁ tiṇhaphalaṁ pāṇinā vā muṭṭhinā vā paṭileṇissāmi paṭikoṭṭissāmi paṭivaṭṭessāmī’ti.
root_text.sn20.5:1.6: bhabbo nu kho so puriso amuṁ sattiṁ tiṇhaphalaṁ pāṇinā vā muṭṭhinā vā paṭileṇetuṁ paṭikoṭṭetuṁ paṭivaṭṭetun”ti?
root_text.sn20.5:1.9: “Asu hi, bhante, satti tiṇhaphalā na sukarā pāṇinā vā muṭṭhinā vā paṭileṇetuṁ paṭikoṭṭetuṁ paṭivaṭṭetuṁ.
root_text.sn20.5:1.10: Yāvadeva ca pana so puriso kilamathassa vighātassa bhāgī assā”ti.
root_text.sn20.5:2.1: “Evameva kho, bhikkhave, yassa kassaci bhikkhuno mettācetovimutti bhāvitā bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā, tassa ce amanusso cittaṁ khipitabbaṁ maññeyya;
root_text.sn20.5:2.2: atha kho sveva amanusso kilamathassa vighātassa bhāgī assa.
root_text.sn20.5:2.4: ‘mettā no cetovimutti bhāvitā bhavissati bahulīkatā yānīkatā vatthukatā anuṭṭhitā paricitā susamāraddhā’ti.

translation_text.sn20.5:1.2: “Mendicants, suppose there was a sharp-pointed spear.
translation_text.sn20.5:1.3: And a man came along and thought,
translation_text.sn20.5:1.4: ‘With my hand or fist I’ll fold this sharp spear over, crumple it, and bend it back!’
translation_text.sn20.5:1.6: Is that man capable of doing so?”
translation_text.sn20.5:1.9: Because it’s not easy to fold that sharp spear over, crumple it, and bend it back with the hand or fist.
translation_text.sn20.5:1.10: That man will eventually get weary and frustrated.”
translation_text.sn20.5:2.1: “In the same way, suppose a mendicant has developed the heart’s release by love, has cultivated it, made it a vehicle and a basis, kept it up, consolidated it, and properly implemented it. Should any non-human think to overthrow their mind,
translation_text.sn20.5:2.2: they’ll eventually get weary and frustrated.
translation_text.sn20.5:2.3: So you should train like this:
translation_text.sn20.5:2.4: ‘We will develop the heart’s release by love. We’ll cultivate it, make it our vehicle and our basis, keep it up, consolidate it, and properly implement it.’
